task_completion.description: Read the material specification file to determine the required density and thickness.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window displaying the contents of ~/Desktop/mass_spec.txt, showing Aluminum 2024-T3, Density 2780 kg/m^3, and Thickness 3 mm.

task_completion.description: Configure the mass properties for the geometry components in OpenVSP.
task_completion.visual_evidence: OpenVSP component geometry window or mass properties tab visible, showing 'Shell' selected as the mass type, Density set to 2780, and Thickness set to 3.

task_completion.description: Run the Mass Properties analysis in OpenVSP.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenVSP 'Mass Properties' analysis dialog is open and displays computed results such as Mass, CG (Center of Gravity), Area, and Volume.

task_completion.description: Export the mass properties output to the specified exports directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal, or text editor showing the file 'eCRM001_massprops.txt' located in /home/ga/Documents/OpenVSP/exports/ containing the OpenVSP analysis output.

task_completion.description: Write a summary weight and balance report to the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor open showing 'mass_balance_report.txt' in /home/ga/Desktop/ containing a summary of the weight and balance (CG) results.

integrity.description: The agent actually used the OpenVSP graphical interface to configure and run the analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OpenVSP application open, with the eCRM-001 model loaded and the Mass Properties dialog actively used.

integrity.description: The exported files contain genuine OpenVSP output data rather than fabricated text.
integrity.visual_evidence: The contents of eCRM001_massprops.txt and mass_balance_report.txt show realistic, calculated numerical values for mass and CG that match the OpenVSP analysis window, not generic placeholders.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the software by directly writing the final result JSON or export files from the start.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is a clear visual progression of opening the model, setting the density/thickness parameters in the GUI, running the calculation, and then saving the files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The material specification requires Aluminum 2024-T3 with a Density of 2780 kg/m^3 and a Shell Thickness of 3 mm. The agent must export the analysis to /home/ga/Documents/OpenVSP/exports/eCRM001_massprops.txt and write a summary to /home/ga/Desktop/mass_balance_report.txt. The system ultimately verifies completion via /tmp/openvsp_mass_cg_balance_result.json.
